title: CertiK CEO: AI-Powered DeFi Attackers Outspending Defenders
summary: DeFi attackers are using AI to exploit security weaknesses beyond smart contract bugs, making it harder for defenders to keep up. The CEO of CertiK warns that this creates an unfair advantage for hackers.
body:
The CEO of CertiK, a leading blockchain security firm, has warned that DeFi attackers are increasingly leveraging AI to outspend and outmaneuver defenders. Instead of focusing solely on smart contract bugs, these attackers are now targeting operational security and supply-chain vulnerabilities.

According to CertiK, the use of AI allows attackers to identify and exploit weaknesses more quickly and efficiently than traditional security measures can address. This shift in tactics has made it increasingly difficult for DeFi platforms to protect themselves, as hackers can adapt and launch attacks faster than defenders can respond.

For everyday users, this means that the security landscape of DeFi is becoming more complex and risky. As attackers become more sophisticated, the potential for financial losses due to hacks and exploits increases. Users must be more vigilant and consider using platforms with robust security measures in place.

Moving forward, DeFi platforms and security firms will need to invest more in advanced AI-driven security solutions to keep pace with attackers. Users should stay informed about the security practices of the platforms they use and consider using multi-signature wallets and other security tools to protect their assets.
